steps.title: Target the Colombian claim
steps.content: The underlined claim says guinea pigs in Puerto Rico probably came from Colombia and descended from Colombian guinea pigs. The passage supports that claim with trade-route evidence: ancient routes connected Puerto Rico with Colombia but not with Peru. To weaken the claim directly, the finding should challenge Colombian ancestry, not merely describe Puerto Rican culture or later guinea pig appearances.

steps.title: Use genetic evidence
steps.content: A says ancient Puerto Rican guinea pigs were genetically less similar to Colombian guinea pigs than to Peruvian guinea pigs, which points away from Colombian descent and toward Peru despite the trade-route evidence. B only shows that guinea pigs appeared often in Puerto Rican art. C compares modern breeds with ancient art, not ancient populations with one another. D makes a Colombian source seem more available, so it does not weaken the claim. The answer is A.
